title: poa glauca subsp. glaucantha
intro: Poa glauca subsp. glaucantha, also known as blue fescue grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Poa glauca subsp. glaucantha include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from its leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of ointments or salves.
